description:
Create an industrial warehouse building with a two-bay sawtooth roof profile and install solar panels on both south-facing sloped roof surfaces using the Skelion plugin. 

1. Set the geographic location to Tucson, Arizona, USA (using SketchUp's geo-location or Skelion settings).
2. Model the warehouse: 24m (east-west) x 16m (north-south) footprint. Perimeter wall height is 5m. The roof has a sawtooth configuration with 2 bays (each 8m wide north-south). Each sawtooth bay has a south-facing slope rising from 5m to 8m, followed by a vertical drop back to 5m. The ridges run east-west.
3. Select the two south-facing sloped roof surfaces.
4. Use Skelion to insert solar panels on these surfaces (Orientation: Landscape, flush-mounted to the sloped surfaces).
5. Save the completed model to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\sawtooth_solar.skp'.
